name: I asked ChatGPT about my car accident case and now I'm suing - can the insurance company get those chats?
description: Yes, AI chats are discoverable and unprotected by privilege per 2026 NY ruling—consult a lawyer now to mitigate risks.[1]

mainEntity.name: I asked ChatGPT about my car accident case and now I'm suing - can the insurance company get those chats?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, AI chats are discoverable and unprotected by privilege per 2026 NY ruling—consult a lawyer now to mitigate risks.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, your AI conversations are likely discoverable in litigation as they don't qualify for attorney-client privilege.[1] A 2026 New York federal court ruling in *United States v. Heppner* held that AI chats fail privilege protections because AI isn't a licensed attorney and conversations aren't confidential.[1] Key factors: AI disclaims legal advice role; prompts can reveal inconsistencies in your story; only protected if a lawyer directs AI use as work product.[1] Delete nothing—preserve all records; consult a lawyer immediately to assess exposure; avoid further solo AI use.[1] Get professional review before any deposition.
